characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: The leader of the titular League, a short red man always seen wearing a helmet. He believes himself to be the greatest evildoer in the world and is very determined to prove this. However, not only are his evil schemes ineffectual at best, but they are constantly foiled by poor planning, bad luck, his own short-sightedness and pettiness, and/or the ineptitude of his minions. Ambiguously Human: Even without his helmet or suit, he's shown to have extremely pale skin and entirely black eyes with glowing yellow pupils. He could be an alien invader of some kind, which would explain a few things. Attention Whore: A lot of his "evil" schemes are attempts to get noticed by the other villains or superheroes. He's actually quite delighted when he has to compete when more competent villains or actual superheroes show up to thwart him, taking it as an indicator of his great villainy. Big Bad Wannabe: Believes himself to be a great villain, though his schemes rarely do more than annoy the neighbors. And that's when they work. He's always upstaged by every other villain, especially Skullossus. Catapult Nightmare: Experiences this in the episodeGolden Claw, after dreaming of Doktor Frogg winning all the prizes in the claw machine to The Cougar’s delight, leaving nothing but a stick of lip gloss.Voltar:[after waking up screaming]Not lip gloss!

characters.name: Voltar
characters.description: The leader of the titular League, a short red man always seen wearing a helmet. He believes himself to be the greatest evildoer in the world and is very determined to prove this. However, not only are his evil schemes ineffectual at best, but they are constantly foiled by poor planning, bad luck, his own short-sightedness and pettiness, and/or the ineptitude of his minions. Ambiguously Human: Even without his helmet or suit, he's shown to have extremely pale skin and entirely black eyes with glowing yellow pupils. He could be an alien invader of some kind, which would explain a few things. Attention Whore: A lot of his "evil" schemes are attempts to get noticed by the other villains or superheroes. He's actually quite delighted when he has to compete when more competent villains or actual superheroes show up to thwart him, taking it as an indicator of his great villainy. Big Bad Wannabe: Believes himself to be a great villain, though his schemes rarely do more than annoy the neighbors. And that's when they work. He's always upstaged by every other villain, especially Skullossus. Catapult Nightmare: Experiences this in the episodeGolden Claw, after dreaming of Doktor Frogg winning all the prizes in the claw machine to The Cougar’s delight, leaving nothing but a stick of lip gloss.Voltar:[after waking up screaming]Not lip gloss!

characters.name: Doktor Frogg
characters.description: The brains of the League, a scrawny man with goggles, a steel chin, and metallic pincers in place of hands. Unlike his teammates, he has genuine plans for world domination and is very capable of engineering machines to do so. Too bad the others don't take him seriously - a problem further exasperated by his constant stream of bad luck and his inability to avoid pain and injury. Affably Evil: Not nearly as much as Red Menace, but he's still fairly polite for anAx-Crazypsychopath bent on world domination. Butt-Monkey: Most of the abuse and physical humour tends to happen to him. Childhood Friends: In "Once Upon a Lose"it is revealed that Red Menace and Doktor Frogg have been best friends since school and have known and followed Voltar for just as long. Deadpan Snarker: Being a supergenius, he is often annoyed by Voltar's idiocy and lack of ambition beyond annoyance.

characters.name: Red Menace
characters.description: The muscle of the League, a large redheaded man in green. He's a very cheerful and kindhearted, if rather naive and childish, individual, and he enjoys such things as baking, gardening, helping little old ladies cross the street, and suggesting moral and friendly ways to resolve problems. In fact, he's not very evil at all, much to the chagrin of his teammates. Action Mom: Not him per say, but rather Red's Mom. She's also a (friendly) supervillain but also very formidable at ass-kicking herself. Affably Evil:Exaggeratedto ridiculous levels, to the point that he has no evil or even jerkish qualities whatsoever but still calls himself evil. Berserk Button: Has this especially in the episodeGameageddon, sporting afeverish steaming red forehead out of rage.After having to go back to the beginning of the board game at one point by bad luck, Red completely loses it,his forehead turns steaming red (akin to a fever)and he starts wrecking the lair in anger, to the belief (as mentioned by Doktor Frogg) that the game was real andblames Voltar for losing the game and starting over, evenas Voltar tries to blame Frogg for it as an excuse.Red Menace:YOUPUT THE HOLEIN MYBASKET!!!Later when Red calms down and casually continues playing the game with the disguised Voltar, Frogg and Doomageddon, he loses his temper again when he finds out their disguises when Voltar accidentally blew their cover.Red Menace:You’re notPUPPIES!!!After winning the board game and suggesting a game of tag, Voltar takes advantage by tagging him first, once again enraging Red into chasing him.Voltar: Tag! You’re it!Red Menace:No…I’m…NOOOOOT!!! After having to go back to the beginning of the board game at one point by bad luck, Red completely loses it,his forehead turns steaming red (akin to a fever)and he starts wrecking the lair in anger, to the belief (as mentioned by Doktor Frogg) that the game was real andblames Voltar for losing the game and starting over, evenas Voltar tries to blame Frogg for it as an

characters.name: Doomageddon
characters.description: The League's pet, a reptilian-canine creature called a Pan-Dimensional Doomhound (Hellhound in "Table for Four"). He possesses the ability to teleport between realities in a flash of flame. He also has a pretty big appetite, which often causes trouble for the League, especially Doktor Frogg, who can never seem to get on his good side. Animalistic Abomination: He's pretty much called a hellhound but he looks more like a cross between a lizard and a monkey. Then there's the fact that he's a pan-dimensional creature with a number of powers and when Doktor Frogg manages to pull rotten tooth out, the hole in his gums turns into a dimensional vacuum. Big Eater: His large appetite has made him a bit of a hindrance for the League's schemes and has swallowed objects several times his size on a few occasions. Extreme Omnivore: He'll eat pretty much anything he can get his jaws on, particularly Doktor Frogg. Hellhound: Actually a"doomhound", but he's a pan-dimensional dog-likeAnimalistic Abominationwhose appetite is as large as Jupiter. Voltar actually calls him one in "Table for Four".

characters.name: Lightning Liz
characters.description: Powered Armor: Nagged her dad into letting her use it. Rich Bitch: As General Sergeant's daughter, she gleefully exploits the wealth his position affords him. Sassy Black Woman: Some of her mannerisms evoke this. Sitcom Arch-Nemesis: To the League in the first episode; she spends the majority of that episode chasing them down for wasting her time. Spoiled Brat: To a tee. Her dad is such a pushover that she can get him and his scientists to give or make her whatever she wants.

characters.name: Justice Gene
characters.description: Butt-Monkey: He dreams of becoming a superhero, except he lacks the brains and competence to be one. In fact, the reason he takes such an interest in the League is that they're the only villain someone as incompetent as him stands a chance against The Ditz: Indisputably the dumbest character in the show, to the point even Voltar is capable of easily outwitting him. Face–Heel Turn: Briefly becameInjustice Gene, which went about as well as his other career choices. Heroic Wannabe: He dreams of becoming a superhero, except he lacks the brains and competence to be one. Serious Business: He took his neighborhood watch job VERY seriously.

characters.name: Skullossus
characters.description: Affably Evil: Despite being the premiere supervillain, he is quite friendly to others outside of his supervillain-ing, even willing to pay for his groceries. Disproportionate Retribution: He got a thing for throwing his Skullmandos off the ship for the smallest things such as not having cream in his coffee, no wet floor sign and a late fee on a DVD. Even Evil Has Loved Ones: He has a son who looks like a miniature version of himself, that Skullosus dotes upon like a loving father. Evil Sounds Deep: He's a powerful supervillain with a deep, commanding voice. Friendly Enemy: With General Sergeant. Despite being Skullossus’ nemesis, the two are still able to have fairly friendly conversations with the General even giving him dating advice.

characters.name: Glory Guy
characters.description: Big Good: He’s hailed as the city’s greatest superhero. Bratty Half-Pint:He used to be a hugeJerkasswho bullied other heroes-in-training alongside Wow Woman and Nightshade when they were kids, until Voltar inspired their victims to fight back and become villains. Create Your Own Villain:Him cheating Voltar out of the chance to become Metrotown’s next big hero led to the formation of the League of Super Evil, with his relentless bullying of Skullossus, Doom Driver, and Rock Gothlington back when they were heroes-in-training also causing them to fall into villainy.

characters.name: Wow Woman
characters.description: All Women Love Shoes: She's a super hero that fights exclusively through the use of super-powered footwear, and she collects them obsessively. Ms. Fanservice: One of the few female characters with a more conventional design, and plenty of attention drawn to her shapely figure, to the point that Voltar, Frogg, and Red usually end up hopelessly smitten with her whenever she's around. The Smurfette Principle: Among the Hall of Glory heroes, she's the only female hero given any prominence.

characters.name: Nightshade
characters.description: Baritone of Strength: He speaks in a low, grumble, akin to Batman inThe Dark Knight Trilogy. Dark Is Not Evil: He sports a black color scheme, but is a superhero.

characters.name: The Cougar
characters.description: Classy Cat-Burglar: Just like her inspiration, if adding on a decade or three. Her deal is stealing valuable items from the bank, while wearing a skin-tight leather cat suit to better fit herAnimal Motif. Dirty Old Woman: She hits on younger villains like the League, much to their disgust (sans Frogg). Evil Old Folks: She's up there in years and is a villain. Expy: OfCatwoman, if Selina Kyle continued being Catwoman long past her sexy, physically capable prime. Meaningful Name: Cougar is a term given to older woman who have relationships with younger men. It's her villain name and she is older, but not asappealing as the term is often associated with.

characters.name: Steve
characters.description: Catchphrase:"I don't care", which he will nonchalantly shout from off screen whenever Voltar mentions him during one of his over the top villain rants. Sitcom Arch-Nemesis: Voltar has a completely one-sided beef with him. The episodeSteeeeve!!!is all about Voltar trying his hardest to get under Steve's perpetually-ambivalent skin.

characters.name: Chuckles
characters.description: Blob Monster: Implied. At one point, Chuckles phases his entire body (except for his hat) through a vertical grate, complete with a slimy sound effect. He is a robot, but this moment implies that he might have a bloblike consistency. Creepy High-Pitched Voice: Chuckles has a silly, high-pitched voice that suits him as aMonster Clown. He makes great use of this voice in hisfrequent, disturbing laughter. Gone Horribly Right: Chuckles was created to pull pranks on children who didn't invite Dr. Frogg to their birthday parties. Chuckles became so good at pulling pranks that he started pranking every person around him, even Dr. Frogg. The Hyena: Being a clown, he laughs very frequently, especially when pulling pranks. Monster Clown: An evil robot clown who takes joy in pulling cruel and potentially deadly pranks on people.
